title: Injection of AAV and Prism GRIN Lens Implantation – Combined Surgery Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"crra1","text":"This protocol describes a combined surgical approach involving AAV injection and prism–GRIN lens implantation in mice.\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7f67","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Autoclave surgery tools.
Setup the necessary equipment including: Stereotaxic frame Injector Warming pad Drill Induction Chamber
Disinfect bench and place a bench pad on the working surface.
Designate a sterile area on the working surface for the sterile material (instruments, suture material, gauze, etc.).
Check the isoflurane levels. Levels visible on the front of the vaporizer.
Set the syringe type (1700), the injection volume and injection speed ( 100 nl /min). Make sure injection mode is set to infusion.
Place the syringe in the injector. The needle of the syringe will be used for leveling the skull.
Clean the Hamilton syringe with sterile water, then 70% ethanol then sterile water again. Make sure it is not clogged and that the plunger can move up and down smoothly.
After anesthetizing the mouse, shave its scalp with a razor. Put it back into the anesthesia chamber if needed.
Apply eye ointment.
Fix the mouse to the stereotaxic frame, making sure it’s stable in all planes.
Cut scalp with a surgical blade. The midline incision goes from between the eyes to right before the ears, such as to expose the fronto-nasal suture, the bregma suture and the lambda suture.
Clean the scalp with betadine, then 70% ethanol then betadine again.

Level skull using the injection needle In the AP plane: less than 50 µm DV difference between Bregma and Lambda, In the ML plane: less than 50 µm DV difference between point +2.0 and –2.0 mm ML to Bregma.

Rinse skull with cold saline then clean it with a cotton tip soaked in hydrogen peroxide to get rid of all conjunctive tissues and dry the skull. This will allow the sutures to appear white and well defined.
Move injection needle to target site according to the AP and ML stereotaxic coordinates of injection ROI. Injection site should be about 250 µm away from imaging plane.
Clean the hole from bone powder and blood, rinsing with cold saline.
Drill a burr hole over the target site.
Raise the needle and mark target site with a fine tip marker.
Lower the injection needle to target DV, at 100 µm/s.
Remeasure the DV coordinates at dura level and calculate corresponding target DV coordinates.
Inject 300 nl at 100 nl /min.
Wait for 00:01:00 .
Raise the injection needle completely out of the brain at the same speed.
Raise the injection needle 500 µm at 100 µm/s and wait for 00:01:00 .
Wait for 00:05:00 .
Move injection needle to target site according to AP and ML stereotaxic coordinates of implant ROI.
Remove dura with a needle hook and irrigate with cold saline.
Drill a 1 mm burr hole over the target site.
Raise the needle and mark target site with a fine tip marker.
Use needle hook or Dumont #5 forceps to remove bone fragments from the hole’s edges.
The hole should be just slightly larger than the lens so that the lens fits right within the hole. Adjust size if necessary.
Clean the hole from bone powder and blood, rinsing with cold saline.

Wet little pieces of gel foam with cold saline and clean the exposed brain surface, very gently. Repeat until the cranial window is clean and clear from any debris or blood. If bleeding persist, place a piece of gel foam soaked in cold saline on it for 00:05:00 to stop the bleeding. Make sure the gel foam piece is very wet when removing it.
Screw the dummy microscope to the ProView Stereotaxic rod and attach the rod to the stereotaxic frame.

Lower the lens very slowly at 10 µm/5 s increments to reach DV target. The lens should have a 1 mm segment over the skull available for adhesive application. The prism lens is sharp enough to go through the brain tissue without the need to create a lens track.
Soak a lens paper with 70% ethanol and clean all the surfaces of the lens.
Gently attach the prism lens integrated to the baseplate to the dummy microscope. Do NOT tighten the screw on the baseplate, the magnets are enough for a stable attachment.
Center the lens over the hole, zero at dura and calculate corresponding DV coordinates of ROI.
Remove any fluid that may have accumulated around the lens using a small gel foam piece. The skull and lens must be completely dry before applying any adhesive.
Grab the baseplate with a needle holder firmly, and slowly raise the stereotax rod to detach the dummy microscope from the baseplate.
Wait for cement to cure, about 00:15:00 . Depending on room temperature, curing time might differ. Make sure the cement is very hard before proceeding.
Apply around the lens and on the skull. As you apply it, it will harden progressively allowing you to build up several layers to reach the integrated baseplate.
Mix metabond using 3 scoops of powder, 7 drops of base and 1 drop of catalyst. The mix will be fluid at first which will allow it to spread nicely on the skull and around the lens.
If needed, apply Kwik-Sil using a 25G needle (or smaller) around the lens at the edge of the hole to protect any exposed brain tissue. Excessive use of Kwik-Sil might decrease stability.
Tighten the screw to secure the cover but be careful to not over tighten. When you feel a resistance, add a quarter turn.
Using forceps pick up the baseplate cover and place it on top of the baseplate with the notch aligned to the baseplate’s screw.
Provide post-op analgesics and anti-inflammatory medicine.
Provide post-op care and close monitoring at least 5 days post-surgery.
Detach the mouse from the stereotax and put it back in its cage on the heating pad.

Wait for it to wake up and start moving. Mouse will have a little bit of trouble adjusting to the weight of the head stage. It should be able to move normally withing an hour post-anesthesia recovery.
creator: Johnson Agniswamy
